stories.body: Iran is weighing a proposed interim agreement with the United States to halt their war, reopen the largely closed Strait of Hormuz, and extend an April ceasefire, with Tehran demanding sanctions relief, access to frozen oil revenues, and broader calm across Lebanon while Washington presses to ease oil price pressure at home; negotiators say a ceremony could come within days even as questions linger over Israel’s compliance, the status of Iran’s nuclear program, and enforcement of the deal’s reported 14-point framework.
stories.headline: US and Iran edge toward a deal to halt their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz

stories.body: Hours after Washington and Tehran announced a preliminary accord meant to end hostilities on all fronts, Israel’s defence minister Israel Katz vowed to keep troops in southern Lebanon indefinitely and promised massive retaliation against Iran if attacked, underscoring deep resistance in Tel Aviv to any linkage between the US-Iran deal and Hezbollah’s battlefield, even as the militia halted fire and Israeli operations tapered amid pressure from allies and domestic critics.
stories.headline: Israel insists it will keep fighting in Lebanon despite US-Iran ceasefire terms

stories.body: A Russian overnight barrage of 70 missiles and 611 drones killed at least 11 people across Ukraine, wounded more than 50, and ignited Kyiv’s 11th-century Dormition Cathedral inside the UNESCO-listed Pechersk Lavra complex, prompting President Volodymyr Zelensky to demand stronger air defenses ahead of the G7 summit while Moscow denied striking the landmark and blamed a malfunctioning Patriot missile.
stories.headline: Russian missile barrage kills civilians and sets Kyiv’s historic cathedral ablaze

stories.body: US Central Command said American forces shot down four Iranian drones targeting Strait of Hormuz shipping and then hit Iranian radar and surveillance sites at Goruk and Qeshm Island, the latest escalation as both countries negotiate a ceasefire that would also require Israeli withdrawals from Lebanon while Iran maintains leverage over the vital chokepoint despite having an estimated fifth of its missile arsenal left.
stories.headline: US strikes Iranian coastal sites after Tehran launches drones near Hormuz

stories.body: NASA briefly ordered five astronauts into their docked Crew Dragon as an ISS air leak in Russia’s Zvezda module doubled to two pounds of air per day, but the agency rescinded the evacuation after Roscosmos paused its plan to saw into the suspected crack, with both sides pledging to coordinate repairs on the station that has never been evacuated in its 27-year history.
stories.headline: NASA reverses ISS evacuation order after assessing worsening air leak

stories.body: In a 52-47 vote, Senate Republicans advanced a bill sending an extra $70 billion to Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol to sustain President Donald Trump’s three-year deportation crackdown while defeating efforts to permanently kill his $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, a pot Democrats call a slush fund for allies even though the Justice Department says it will stay dormant.
stories.headline: Senate approves $70 billion for ICE while leaving Trump’s ‘anti-weaponization’ fund intact

stories.body: Former prime minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero has asked Judge José Luis Calama to postpone only the portion of this week’s National Court appearance covering contraband and tax-fraud allegations tied to €1.3 million in undeclared jewels found in his Ferraz office, saying he needs time to gather provenance documents and commission his own appraisal, though he remains willing to testify about the Plus Ultra airline rescue probe.
stories.headline: Zapatero asks judge to delay testimony on €1.3 million in jewels seized from his office

stories.body: A new Guardia Civil UCO report says former PSOE organization chief Santos Cerdán verbally ordered staff to automatically authorize and finance investigator Leire Díez’s travel, car rentals, and legal hires so she could seek compromising information on probes affecting the party, with seized invoices and internal memos showing at least €53,000 in payments to lawyer Jacobo Teijelo outside official party records.
stories.headline: Guardia Civil links PSOE’s Santos Cerdán to orders funding Leire Díez’s covert trips

stories.body: Spain’s coalition partners have revived Sumar’s shelved 2023 bill to reform the penal code by repealing articles that criminalize insults to the Crown, parliament, high state institutions, national symbols, and religious sentiments, while leaving terrorism glorification untouched, with leaders Enrique Santiago, Artemi Rallo, and Culture Minister Ernest Urtasun arguing the changes will better protect satire and free expression and be approved before summer.
stories.headline: PSOE and Sumar agree to decriminalize insults to the Crown and religious offenses

stories.body: Vox spokesman José Antonio Fúster said the party will back Andalusian PP leader Juan Manuel Moreno’s re-election only if he implements its “national priority” platform on issues such as immigration and cultural policy, stressing that Vox is focused on policy guarantees rather than securing cabinet posts but is ready to join the government if needed to enforce deadlines.
stories.headline: Vox conditions support for Moreno’s investiture on adopting a ‘national priority’ agenda

stories.body: Judge Juan Carlos Peinado has ordered Begoña Gómez, her adviser Cristina Álvarez, and businessman Juan Carlos Barrabés to appear in person at a June 15 preliminary hearing required before sending their corruption, malfeasance, influence-peddling, and trademark misappropriation case to a Madrid jury trial, warning that failure to attend could trigger police escort and allowing accusers to seek passport seizures and fortnightly court check-ins.
stories.headline: Judge summons Begoña Gómez again as preliminary hearing brings trial closer

stories.body: Barcelona Mayor Jaume Collboni has enlisted 192 city employees—two-thirds of them women from social services—to volunteer up to four paid hours per month providing remote technical assistance and public-awareness work for the District 11 cooperation program with Gaza, Ramallah, and Bethlehem, matching municipal expertise in areas like urban planning and social care with Palestinian needs while also opening off-hours opportunities for cultural and solidarity events.
stories.headline: Barcelona redeploys 192 municipal staffers to support the ‘District 11’ partnership with Palestinian cities

stories.body: Barcelona’s Grand Départ hosting duties on July 2, 4, and 5 will shut six districts and effectively seal off the Sagrada Família area as team presentations occupy Carrer de la Marina and Avinguda de Gaudí, forcing the basilica and Sant Pau complex to close, removing nearby restaurant terraces, and managing crowds of up to 90,000 before time trials and stage finishes later in the week cost the city an estimated €9.7 million excluding policing and mobility expenses.
stories.headline: Tour de France will trigger sweeping traffic closures that isolate the Sagrada Família area

stories.body: TMB and the Catalan government will use summer to overhaul metro infrastructure, closing L1 between Santa Eulàlia and Mercat Nou with substitute buses every 4–11 minutes, suspending L9/L10 Nord service between La Sagrera and Bon Pastor during two phases backed by shuttle buses, renovating the Catalunya station transfer tunnel, and shutting Verdaguer’s L4 access from July 6 to August 30, while other upgrades target the L9/10 macropit and accessibility works.
stories.headline: Summer works will cut three tram lines and key metro stretches in Barcelona
